configuration.type.transparent.descripti: Make the background transparent, without blur
configuration.opacity.description: Opacity of Vibrancy Effect. -1 is theme default.
configuration.backgroundOverride.descrip: Override the theme's background color for the vibrancy opacity effect. Leave empty to use the theme default.
configuration.enableAutoTheme.descriptio: Enable automatic dark/light mode switching based on OS mode. Requires "window.autoDetectColorScheme" to also be enabled.
configuration.imports.description: Import CSS/JS files, as file paths.
configuration.refreshInterval.descriptio: Refresh interval (in milliseconds) for making the background transparent after window resizing. Lower values make the update less visible at the cost of increased CPU utilization.
configuration.preventFlash.description: Use an alternative method to prevent window flashing when resizing. Eliminates the need for a refresh interval, but may be less compatible in some cases.
configuration.forceFramelessWindow.descr: Always set the VSCode window to use `frame: false`. This is already the default on Windows, so you normally don't need it there — it's mainly an override for editors/platforms where the frameless window isn't applied automatically but is needed to fix rendering issues. On macOS it's off by default: enabling it can stop occasional flashing on file-browser hover, but it raises WindowServer GPU/power usage on recent macOS, so it's not guaranteed to be worthwhile.
configuration.disableFramelessWindow.des: Legacy/troubleshooting setting. The frameless window mitigates a hardware-acceleration render bug seen on some VSCode/Electron versions and GPUs. On current VSCode versions it no longer interferes with window interaction, so most users never need this. Enable it if the frameless window stops you resizing/snapping the window; on an affected GPU you may then see distorted graphics unless you also launch VSCode with --disable-gpu-compositing.
configuration.disableThemeFixes.descript: Disable fixes for non-standard VSCode editors like Cursor.
configuration.disableColorCustomizations: Prevent Vibrancy from modifying `workbench.colorCustomizations`. Enable this if you manage color customizations yourself or use a custom CSS theme that handles its own backgrounds.
extension.installVibrancy.title: Enable Vibrancy
extension.uninstallVibrancy.title: Disable Vibrancy
extension.updateVibrancy.title: Reload Vibrancy
messages.firstload: Welcome to Vibrancy. Please make sure to read the extension's description. Click the button below to install.
messages.updateNeeded: Vibrancy needs to be updated. Click the button below to update.
messages.configupdate: Config is changed, would you like to reload?
messages.enabled: Vibrancy enabled. Restart to take effect. If Code complains about a corrupted installation, CLICK DON'T SHOW AGAIN. See description for more details.
messages.disabled: Vibrancy disabled and reverted to default. Restart to take effect.
messages.unsupported: Unsupported operating system. Only macOS, Linux, and Windows 10/11 on x64 or ARM64 are supported.
messages.unsupportedEditor: Your code editor is not supported, see extension description for the list of supported editors
messages.forceFramelessUnsupportedEditor: A frameless window can't be enabled on this unsupported editor. Disable the "vscode_vibrancy.forceFramelessWindow" setting to install Vibrancy here.
messages.admin: Apply changes failed, please run Visual Studio Code with administrator privileges, see description for more details:
messages.recommendedColorTheme: Your current color theme is "%1" and the Vibrancy theme is designed for "%2"!
messages.htmlError: The workbench.html file is different from what is expected. Please ensure that you don't have other extensions that modify the workbench.html file.
messages.electronDeprecatedType: The "%1" type is deprecated in this version of VSCode. Please update your configuration to use a supported type.
messages.applyToAllProfiles: Your `workbench.settings.applyToAllProfiles` setting has been updated to remove `terminal.background`. This is a one-time change to better support VSCode profiles.
messages.elevationRequired: VSCode installation files require administrator privileges to modify. Grant elevated access?
messages.elevationFailed: Failed to acquire elevated privileges. See Known Errors for manual instructions:
messages.pkexecMissing: polkit (pkexec) is not installed. Install it or manually change file ownership with: sudo chown -R $(whoami)
messages.snapImmutable: VSCode installed via Snap has an immutable filesystem that cannot be modified even with elevated privileges. Please install VSCode via .deb, .rpm, or Flatpak instead.
messages.noNewPrivs: Elevated privileges are unavailable because VSCode was reloaded in-process. Please close VSCode completely and reopen it, then try again.
messages.windowsSudoMissing: This system-level VSCode installation requires administrator privileges. Please enable Windows sudo (Settings → System → For Developers → Enable sudo) or run VSCode as administrator.
